title: BUG #17741: vacuum process hangs after pg_surgery manipulations
description:
The following bug has been logged on the website:
Bug reference:      17741
Logged by:          Alexander Kozhemyakin
Email address:      a(dot)kozhemyakin(at)postgrespro(dot)ru
PostgreSQL version: 15.1
Operating system:   Debian 10 (x86_64)
Description:
On the REL_15_STABLE, you can hang vacuum freeze. Maybe this is not
desired?
https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/pgsurgery.html
reproduce script:
create extension pg_surgery;
create table t1(a int);
insert into t1 values (1);
update t1 set a = 2;
select heap_force_kill('t1'::regclass, ARRAY['(0, 1)']::tid[]);
select ctid from t1;
update t1 set a = 3;
select ctid from t1;
vacuum freeze t1;
Now we have hung vacuum process.

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, value DECIMAL CHECK (value >= 0));
UPDATE t2 SET value = NULL WHERE name = 'test';
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE value IS NULL;
DELETE FROM t2 WHERE value IS NULL;
key_bug_features: vacuum freeze hangs after heap_force_kill manipulation
